parentQuestion: The area of the cortex that is responsible for sensing a full bladder and the fe...

scaffolds.q: The brain can sense internal conditions of the body, not just external stimuli.
scaffolds.explain: In addition to sensing the external world (touch, sight, sound), the brain monitors internal body states like hunger, thirst, bladder fullness, and organ function. This is called interoception — the sense of the body sensing itself.

scaffolds.q: Sensations from internal organs like the stomach, bladder, and lungs are called:
scaffolds.explain: Visceral sensations arise from internal organs (viscera). These include feelings of fullness, hunger, nausea, bladder urgency, and air hunger — the rising panic when you can't breathe.
